frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment:
A: Every time women talk like all men are unsafe, it feels a bit unfair too. Main bhi daily work karke ghar aati hoon, and I know plenty of decent men in office and family.
B: But women are scared for a reason.
A: Scared hona alag baat hai, but assuming the worst from every man is not right yaar. Good men also feel judged for no reason.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's safety concerns by centering the feelings of 'good men' and framing women's caution as unfair generalization. It encodes the 'not all men' response and the idea that women should not be wary of men as a group.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
